Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The logs describe the same order Order1234 Log A has fewer events Order Received Order Validated Payment Processed Item Shipped Item Delivered Log B has OrderReceived OrderValidation PaymentCheck Quality Check Shipping

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Log B has an extra event Quality Check that is not in Log A Also Log A has Item Delivered which is not in Log B

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Log B timestamps are slightly earlier or later Log A Order Received at 100000Z Log B OrderReceived at 095958Z Order Validated 100130Z vs 100129Z Payment Processed 100200Z vs PaymentCheck 100205Z Item Shipped 100500Z vs Shipping 100502Z

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to merge events that represent the same occurrence The matching criteria based on order_id and event timing with tolerance under 2 seconds and naming variations

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Order Received vs OrderReceived Order Validated vs OrderValidation Payment Processed vs PaymentCheck Item Shipped vs Shipping So we can match them accordingly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Quality Check only in B no match in A so include as separate event Item Delivered only in A no match in B so include as separate event

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Probably we can include both timestamps as separate fields or choose the primary based on some rule maybe the primary timeline is Log A It says Log A is considered the primary timeline so maybe we can use Log A timestamps as primary for merged events but also include Log B timestamp as an attribute

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Since Log A is primary timeline we can use its timestamps for ordering For events only in Log B we can use Log B timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Order Received A vs OrderReceived B timestamps differ by 2 seconds They are clearly the same event so I would match them

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Order Validated A vs OrderValidation B timestamps differ by 1 second within tolerance Match

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment Processed A vs PaymentCheck B timestamps differ by 5 seconds which is outside tolerance However they likely represent the same event because both are payment related and there is no other candidate

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: If PaymentCheck is not the same as Payment Processed then what does Payment Processed in A correspond to in B The only candidate is PaymentCheck So it is highly likely that PaymentCheck and Payment Processed are the same event

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The note Payment gateway delay might explain why the timestamp is later so I would match them and document that we are matching despite the 5 second difference

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Shipping vs Item Shipped differ by 2 seconds and are clearly the same activity so we should match them

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For activities that appear in both we use a common name maybe from A Order Received Order Validated Payment Processed Item Shipped For unmatched Quality Check and Item Delivered keep those names

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Using primary timestamps from Log A the order is Order Received at 100000 Order Validated at 100130 Payment Processed at 100200 Quality Check at 100300 Item Shipped at 100500 Item Delivered at 102000

Name: Validation - PE
Text: After merging we have 6 events 4 merged events plus 2 single source events accounting for all original events
